<reasoning>
Use induction on the list. The recursive step is exactly the standard split on whether `x ≤ y`; the provided lemma [L15] packages this induction principle for `sorted` and `insort`, so we can apply it directly after proving the base case and recursive case by `simp`.
</reasoning>

<used_lemmas>
15
</used_lemmas>

<lean4_proof>
induction xs with
| nil =>
    simp [insort]
| cons y ys ih =>
    by_cases hxy : x ≤ y
    · simp [insort, hxy, h]
    · simp [insort, hxy, h, ih]
</lean4_proof>
